perturbation.original_text: 5.5 Expenses and Travel. Any pre-approved expenses incurred by Dr. Murray on behalf of EHS or EHN during any Services Term will be reimbursed, including but not limited to travel expenses incurred for air travel, car rental, hotels and meals, subject to prior approval in each case. EHS or EHN agrees to reimburse Dr. Murray for all reasonable expenses (air travel, hotel, car rental, meals, materials, etc.) relating to EHS or EHN directed activities, subject to prior approval in each case.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 Expenses and Travel. Any expenses incurred by Dr. Murray on behalf of EHS or EHN during any Services Term *may* be reimbursed, only including travel expenses for lodging and meals, subject to *final* approval by both EHS *and* EHN in each case. EHS or EHN agrees to reimburse Dr. Murray for *only* pre-approved travel and lodging expenses (air travel, hotel, car rental, meals, materials, etc.) relating to EHS or EHN directed activities, subject to prior approval in each case.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that any pre-approved expenses 'will be reimbursed', implying a firm commitment. The modified text uses 'may be reimbursed', and introduces final approval from both entities for lodging and meals specifically. The removal of ‘air travel, car rental’ expenses, alongside needing both entities' approval creates ambiguity because other sections of the contract still refer to the original understanding of reimbursement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5 Expenses and Travel. Any expenses incurred by Dr. Murray on behalf of EHS or EHN during any Services Term *may* be reimbursed, only including travel expenses for lodging and meals, subject to *final* approval by both EHS *and* EHN in each case. EHS or EHN agrees to reimburse Dr. Murray for *only* pre-approved travel and lodging expenses (air travel, hotel, car rental, meals, materials, etc.) relating to EHS or EHN directed activities, subject to prior approval in each case.
perturbation.justification: The original text states expenses 'will be reimbursed', creating an obligation, whereas the changed text modifies this to 'may be reimbursed', removing the obligation and introducing ambiguity. Additionally, the original text includes 'air travel, car rental', while the changed text restricts the reimbursement to 'lodging and meals' while the second sentence includes 'air travel, hotel, car rental, meals, materials, etc.' The contradicted section then contradicts itself. This constitutes a direct and impactful contradiction that would likely cause confusion regarding expense reimbursement. The change follows the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: (b) If this Agreement is termination in accordance with Section 6.2 by Dr. Murray, all of the rights and obligations hereunder shall cease and be of no further force or effect, except that Sections 4, 5 (to the extent obligations have accrued prior to termination), 7, 8 and 9 shall survive.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) If this Agreement is termination in accordance with Section 6.2 by Dr. Murray, all of the rights and obligations hereunder shall cease and be of no further force or effect, except that Sections 4, 5 (to the extent obligations have accrued prior to termination), 5.3, 7, 8 and 9 shall survive.
perturbation.explanation: By including 5.3 in the survival clause, after Dr. Murray terminates the contract, there is a contradiction because previously only obligations accrued before termination were valid, which could be interpreted as royalties earned by Dr. Murray until the termination date, whereas this change attempts to make royalty payments continue. Article 6.3(b) now contradicts the understanding established in Article 5.3 about the conditions of royalty payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 Royalty/Commission Payments. Dr. Murray will receive an annual royalty on net sales (defined as gross sales before any deductions or returns) for any products (the "Dr. Murray Products") developed by Dr. Murray for EHN for as long as the Dr. Murray Products are being sold. The Dr. Murray Products will be listed on Schedule A attached hereto as they are developed and added to product portfolio. During each year of this agreement, Dr. Murray will be paid no later than the 30st day of January based on the cumulative Net Sales of the Dr. Murray Products for the preceding 12 months based upon the following scale: Net Sales per 12 Months Royalty $2,000,001 to $3,500,000 $150,000 $3,500,001 to $5,000,000 $250,000 $5,000,001 to $10,000,000 $350,000 $10,000,001 to $25,000,000 $500,000 $25,000,001 to $50,000,000 $1,000,000 $50,000,001 to $75,000,000 $1,500,000 $75,000,001 to $100,000,000 $2,000,000 Greater than $100,000,000 $2,500,000
perturbation.justification: The addition of Section 5.3 to the survival clause in Section 6.3(b) creates a direct contradiction. Originally, only obligations accrued prior to termination under Section 5 survived. However, Section 5.3, which dictates royalty payments for as long as Dr. Murray Products are sold, implies ongoing royalty obligations even after termination. This directly conflicts with the initial understanding that only accrued obligations survive termination. This is an ambiguities-in-text contradiction.
